Antonio Conte has endorsed Chelsea’s decision to let 43-goal Liverpool forward Mohamed Salah leave, insisting he was “a different player” four years ago.

Salah tormented Chelsea while playing for Swiss side Basel and moved to Stamford Bridge in January 2014.

Limited opportunities saw the Egypt forward join Fiorentina on loan 12 months later, and then Roma on a temporary deal which became permanent.

Some have used the benefit of hindsight to criticise Jose Mourinho, now at Manchester United but the manager of Chelsea at the time, as Salah has been thriving for Liverpool since returning to the Premier League from Serie A last summer.

“It’s not so simple,” Chelsea head coach Conte said.

“We have to compare Salah of four, five years ago, and not with this Salah now.

“Salah in the present has improved a lot, he has developed his football a lot.

“I think the Italy experience was very important for him and now I’m sure that he’s a different player, compared to the past.

“For this reason I think it’s very difficult to have this comparison.”

Maurizio Sarri open to Premier League move if he cannot agree new Napoli contract

Napoli boss Maurizio Sarri is thought to be eyeing a move to the Premier League if he cannot agree a new deal with the Italian club.

Sarri, who has been linked with Chelsea if Antonio Conte leaves the club at the end of the season, has a clause in his contract that means he can be ‘signed’ for £7million if Napoli receive a formal approach from another club before the end of May.

Sarri’s annual salary at Napoli, who are chasing their first Serie A title since 1990, is £1.3million and he is keen to boost those earnings significantly when he signs his next contract.

Sarri has strong links to the Napoli fans but wants guarantees that money will be spent of improving an ageing squad before he commits himself to the club again.

Conte’s Chelsea face Swansea on Saturday knowing a win will keep their top-four hopes alive, as Tottenham are just five points ahead and do not play until Monday.

I told them [my team-mates], look guys, I’ve been unbeaten here and I want this moment to carry on. What a great feeling it is to win the FA Cup, I want them to know this. It has been a tough season but I want to finish strong.”

“Competition is always good, especially for strikers,” he said. “It helps to step up and help the team win the games. It’s a fair competition. We’ve done well with Alvaro when we played Burnley, we’ve done well with Eden, with Willy, with Pedro. Whoever is playing, we always try to combine to do the work the boss is expecting us to do.”

【now.com體育】穆罕默德沙拿疑似在上場英超拳打馬田斯恩迪，有機會被英格蘭足總罰停賽3場，令哈利卡尼更有機會後上贏得金靴獎。

利物浦在上場英超主場只能跟史篤城踢成0:0，大勇翼鋒穆罕默德沙拿（Mohamed Salah）在半場前跟史篤城中堅馬田斯恩迪鬥法時，用左手向對方打了一下；球證當時未有目睹事件，但被攝影機拍下，而且片段於網上流傳。

英格蘭足總將會就事件作出調查，如果由3名前球證組成的小組認為沙拿有罪，勢以暴力行為罰這名埃及國腳停賽3場；由於利物浦今季只餘下2場本土賽事，因此將會提早收咧，而下季第1場本土賽事亦要坐波監。

沙拿現時在英超射手榜以31球排第1位，領先次席熱刺前鋒哈利卡尼5球，後者希望連續3季拿下金靴獎之心，路人皆見；要是沙拿停賽，卡尼要在餘下賽事反先對手，不是沒有可能。

Chelsea interested in making move for Manchester United’s Anthony Martial

Chelsea have joined the list of clubs monitoring Anthony Martial’s situation at Manchester United and would move for the France international if the striker agitates for a transfer from Old Trafford this summer.

Martial has endured a frustrating third campaign in English football, flitting from team to bench and limited to 17 Premier League starts, and suitors – who already included Juventus and Tottenham Hotspur – have been granted enough encouragement that he could seek a fresh start at the end of the season. The Frenchman is contracted to 2019 with an option for a further year, and would therefore command a considerable fee. Juve are understood to have indicated they would be prepared to pay around £45m for his services.

Chelsea, braced for Real Madrid to firm up their long-standing interest in Eden Hazard, would consider the 22-year-old a player of considerable potential, but also one with the quality already to feature anywhere across their front line. United’s instinct will be to retain the forward, who they secured from Monaco for an initial £36m in 2015, but, if Martial pushes for a move, they may not be averse to selling, even to a divisional rival. The clubs negotiated Nemanja Matic’s move to Old Trafford last summer, and José Mourinho retains an interest in the Chelsea forward, Willian.
